Header file for launching and exiting JerryScript on Mbed OS.

# Purpose
This code is a C header file that declares two functions: [`jsmbed_js_launch`](<#jsmbed_js_launch>) and [`jsmbed_js_exit`](<#jsmbed_js_exit>). These functions are likely intended to manage the lifecycle of a JavaScript engine or environment, specifically for the JerryScript engine on an mbed platform. The [`jsmbed_js_launch`](<#jsmbed_js_launch>) function is expected to initialize or start the JavaScript environment, while the [`jsmbed_js_exit`](<#jsmbed_js_exit>) function is expected to terminate or clean up the environment. The header file uses include guards to prevent multiple inclusions, ensuring that the function declarations are only included once in a compilation unit.

### jsmbed\_js\_launch<!-- {{#callable_declaration:jsmbed_js_launch}} -->

Initializes and starts the JerryScript engine on mbed.
- **Description**: Use this function to initialize the JerryScript engine and start executing JavaScript code on an mbed platform. It first initializes the necessary components and then attempts to load JavaScript code. If the code loads successfully, it enters the event loop to execute the code. This function must be called in an environment where the mbed platform is properly set up and ready to execute JavaScript.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: None

### jsmbed\_js\_exit<!-- {{#callable_declaration:jsmbed_js_exit}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../../../src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/targets/mbedos5/jerryscript-mbed/jerryscript-mbed-launcher/launcher.h#L19>)

Cleans up the JerryScript engine environment.
- **Description**: Use this function to release resources and perform cleanup operations for the JerryScript engine. Call it when the engine is no longer needed, typically at the end of the program or before shutting down the system. Ensure that no further operations on the JerryScript engine are performed after calling this function, as it finalizes the engine's state.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: None
